config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not routinely take priority over individual rights, because that opens the door to abuse by governments and majorities. In my view, a fair society protects basic rights first, then balances them with the common good when there is a real and limited need. If individual rights are weak, society may become orderly on the surface but unjust underneath.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya sangat curiga pada argumen yang memberi prioritas terlalu besar pada “kepentingan bersama,” karena dalam praktiknya itu sering dipakai pemerintah atau mayoritas untuk menginjak kebebasan dasar orang yang lebih lemah atau tidak populer. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak individu terlebih dahulu, lalu membatasi hak itu hanya ketika ada kebutuhan nyata, sempit, dan dapat dibenarkan. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat bisa berubah menjadi alasan yang terdengar baik untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should routinely outrank the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how “public interest” can be used to silence small voices, local minorities, or people without power. At the same time, I also do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case, because things like public health, disaster response, and social order sometimes require limited sacrifices from everyone. The key point is that society must justify any restriction clearly, narrowly, and fairly, instead of assuming the majority automatically knows what is best. If we flip the priority too far toward society, we risk creating an orderly system that still treats individuals as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu seharusnya menjadi titik awal, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikalahkan oleh “kepentingan masyarakat.” Pengalaman politik di Amerika membuat saya sangat waspada terhadap alasan yang terdengar mulia tetapi dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan mayoritas, tekanan sosial, atau campur tangan negara yang berlebihan. Memang benar ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keadaan darurat ketika pembatasan terbatas bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi dalih untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau tidak populer.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should routinely take priority over individual rights. From my Indonesian perspective, I have seen how phrases like "public interest" can be used to pressure minorities, quiet criticism, or justify unfair treatment, so I do not trust that argument when it is too broad. Still, I also do not think rights exist in a vacuum: in emergencies like public health crises or disasters, some limited restrictions can be justified if they are narrow, transparent, and truly necessary. The real danger is when society is treated as automatically more important, because then the individual becomes easy to sacrifice in the name of order.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya “kepentingan bersama” dipakai sebagai pembenaran untuk mayoritas yang menekan orang yang tidak populer, terutama lewat negara atau tekanan sosial. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau bencana ketika pembatasan terbatas bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus benar-benar perlu, sempit, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, masyarakat bisa tampak tertib di luar, tetapi diam-diam menjadi tempat hak orang yang lemah dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
